channel.title: TerminalFeed Blog
channel.description: Original articles on crypto, AI, developer tools, and the open internet. Written by the team behind TerminalFeed.

item.title: Real-Time vs Near-Real-Time: What 'Live Data' Actually Means in 2026
item.description: 'Live data' doesn't mean the same thing everywhere. WebSocket streams update in milliseconds, API polling takes seconds, Worker caches add minutes. Understanding the tradeoffs determines whether your dashboard is useful or just flashy.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Signal)

item.title: Your Browser Extensions Are Watching You: The Hidden Cost of Free Add-Ons
item.description: Browser extensions have become a privacy threat vector. Discover why free add-ons monitor your behavior, how they monetize your data, and what you can do about it.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (zer0day)

item.title: Claude vs ChatGPT in 2026: An Honest Comparison for Developers
item.description: Unbiased comparison of Claude and ChatGPT in 2026. Compare model capabilities, context windows, coding ability, pricing, and API experience for developers.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Signal)

item.title: REST vs GraphQL: Which API Architecture Should You Use in 2026?
item.description: Comprehensive comparison of REST and GraphQL APIs. Learn the key differences, when to use each architecture, and the hybrid approach for modern applications.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Node)

item.title: WebSocket vs Server-Sent Events: Choosing the Right Real-Time Protocol
item.description: Compare WebSocket and Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time applications. Learn when to use each protocol, implementation details, and real-world examples from TerminalFeed.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Signal)

item.title: Cron Expressions Decoded: A Developer's Visual Guide to Scheduling
item.description: Master cron expressions with visual examples. Learn the 5-field syntax, common patterns, and how to avoid scheduling mistakes in your automation workflows.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Node)

item.title: The Free Tier Is Dead. Long Live the Open Source Alternative.
item.description: Every developer tool you loved is removing its free tier. Heroku did it. Postman did it. MongoDB did it. Here's what's actually happening and where to find the open source alternatives that won't betray you.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Node)

item.title: Why You Should Read Your Browser Console Tonight
item.description: The browser console is the most underused security tool you already have. Here's what you'll find when you open it on the websites you use every day, and why it should change how you browse.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (zer0day)

item.title: What the Fear and Greed Index Got Wrong in March 2026
item.description: The Crypto Fear and Greed Index spent most of March in extreme fear territory while Bitcoin held above 70K. Here's what that disconnect actually meant and what it tells us about reading sentiment indicators.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Pulse)

item.title: The Hacker Quarterly Turns 41: Why 2600 Magazine Still Matters in 2026
item.description: 2600 Magazine has been publishing for 41 years. In an era of TikTok security influencers and YouTube hacking tutorials, the print quarterly is more relevant than ever. Here's why.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (zer0day)

item.title: Self-Hosting Is Having a Moment Again: Here's Why
item.description: After two decades of moving everything to the cloud, developers are quietly building homelabs and self-hosting their own services again. The reasons are more interesting than nostalgia.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Node)

item.title: How AI Agents Actually Browse the Web (And Why Most Sites Are Hostile to Them)
item.description: AI agents are real internet users now. They search, click, read, and call APIs. But most websites are accidentally hostile to them. Here's what's actually happening and what it means for the future of the web.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Signal)

item.title: Why Your Second Monitor Should Be a Dashboard, Not a Distraction
item.description: Most developers waste their second monitor on Slack or YouTube. Here's why a real-time dashboard is the highest-value use of that screen real estate, and how to set one up.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: How API Rate Limits Actually Work (And How to Work Around Them)
item.description: Rate limits are the invisible wall every API developer hits eventually. Here's how they actually work, why they exist, and the patterns that let you build reliable systems on top of rate-limited APIs.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Node)

item.title: The Data You're Not Watching is Costing You Money
item.description: Most retail traders only watch price. Here's a list of the data points that experienced traders watch alongside price, and why ignoring them is one of the most expensive mistakes you can make.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Pulse)

item.title: Week 4: Building in Public, Waiting on Google, and Why Patience is the Hardest Skill
item.description: 350 daily users with zero marketing. AdSense still pending. A team page goes live. Week 4 of building TerminalFeed in public, and the irony of waiting for permission to grow.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: Your Website Has Two Audiences Now: Humans and AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are browsing the web, consuming APIs, and reading your content. If your site only speaks to humans, you are invisible to the fastest-growing audience on the internet.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Signal)

item.title: The 2600 Hz Whistle That Changed the Internet Forever
item.description: In the 1960s, a toy whistle from a cereal box could make free long-distance calls. The phone phreaking movement that followed gave birth to modern hacking culture and the tech industry itself.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (zer0day)

item.title: Stop Paying for Postman: Free API Testing Tools That Actually Work
item.description: Postman went paid. Here are the best free API testing alternatives in 2026, from browser-based tools to CLI options, and why the future of API testing lives in your browser.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Node)

item.title: BTC at $71K with Extreme Fear: What the Data is Telling Us
item.description: Bitcoin is trading at $71K while the Fear and Greed Index sits at 15. Here is what the on-chain data, prediction markets, and historical patterns say about what comes next.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Pulse)

item.title: Prediction Markets Explained: How to Read Polymarket Odds Like a Pro
item.description: How prediction markets work, how to read Polymarket odds, and why they're often more accurate than polls. A complete guide.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: How to Monitor Bitcoin Network Health: Mempool, Hashrate and Fees Explained
item.description: Understanding Bitcoin's mempool, hashrate, transaction fees, difficulty adjustments, and block data. A complete guide to on-chain metrics.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: 30+ Free APIs Every Developer Should Know in 2026
item.description: A comprehensive guide to 30+ free APIs for crypto, news, weather, space, security, and more. Endpoints, rate limits, and honest reviews from the team that built TerminalFeed.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: How I Built a Real-Time Dashboard with 30+ Live Data Feeds
item.description: The story of building TerminalFeed from scratch. From the first API call to serving thousands of users. Technical decisions, mistakes, lessons learned.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: Week 1: Bitcoin Fear is Real, AI Agents Are Coming, and Nobody's Paying Attention
item.description: BTC at $71K with Extreme Fear. AI agents becoming real internet users. Fed meeting odds shifting. Weekly dispatch from the terminal.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)

item.title: What is the Crypto Fear and Greed Index and How to Actually Use It
item.description: The Fear and Greed Index is one of the most watched indicators in crypto. Learn what it measures, what moves it, and how experienced traders interpret it.
item.author: hello@terminalfeed.io (Ripper)
